message:
feat(demo_showcase): implement Dashboard layout with metrics and sparklines

- Status bar showing platform health, service summary, uptime
- Stats row with 4 metric cards (services, active jobs, completed, deploys)
- Service health list with status chips and version info
- Recent deployments with status badges and commit authors
- Recent jobs with progress indicators
- Simple sparkline chart for request rate visualization
- Real data integration with GeneratedData
- Support for deterministic rendering via seed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-i0i9","title":"Docs: docs navigation (list of pages + preview)","description":"Implement a docs index + navigation experience.

Design:
- Split view:
  - Left pane: doc page list (filterable list)
  - Right pane: rendered content (glamour + viewport)

Behavior:
- Switching docs is instant.
- Preserve per-doc scroll position when switching away and back (optional but high polish).
- The list should reflect the authored docs content (title/subtitle) rather than raw filenames.

Integration:
- Hooks into in-doc search (search operates within the current doc).

Acceptance:
- Navigation feels fast and "real product" quality.
- Selection and (optionally) scroll state are preserved across page navigations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.361710616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:29.373781335Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","list"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.387706158Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:26.023997036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iwbw","title":"Dashboard: animated counters/progress (harmonica)","description":"Make the Dashboard feel alive with tasteful, physics-based motion (harmonica).

Why:
- Smooth motion is a signature Charm aesthetic.
- Animated counters and progress bars make metrics changes readable and satisfying.

What to animate:
- Counter values (requests/sec, latency, error rate):
  - spring toward target to avoid jitter
- Progress bars:
  - ease changes rather than snapping
- Optional:
  - a subtle transition when switching focus between dashboard cards

Constraints:
- Must not interfere with input responsiveness.
- Must respect reduce-motion / `--no-animations`:
  - values snap directly to targets

Acceptance:
- Motion is smooth, stable, and never causes flicker.
- The dashboard remains readable even with rapid metric changes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.093043228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:09.317467932Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","harmonica"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-2myg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:19.483793101Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:20.705038373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.109088230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k52c","title":"demo_showcase theme: live theme switching UI","description":"Implement runtime theme switching.

Requirements:
- A theme picker (modal or Settings page) showing available presets.
- Instant visual update across the whole app (ThemeContext listeners).
- Store selected theme in app state; optional: persist to a small config file.

 {"id":"bd-lxky","title":"Unit tests: animation subsystem determinism + reduce-motion","description":"Add deterministic unit tests for the demo_showcase animation subsystem.

Why:
- The showcase will lean heavily on motion; we need confidence it won’t become flaky or timing-dependent.
- CI must be able to validate animation code paths without real sleeps.

Test coverage:
- Given a fixed dt (e.g., 16ms) and fixed initial/target values, stepping the animation yields deterministic values.
- Animations stay bounded (no NaNs, no runaway values, no panics on extreme dt).
- Reduce-motion / `--no-animations` behavior:
  - animated values immediately snap to target (or the subsystem becomes a no-op) according to the design.
  - no tick scheduling when animations are disabled.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not depend on wall-clock time.
- Failures clearly report which animation invariant broke.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.665155320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688983950Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688967780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.679805544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.684774144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-miqo","title":"E2E scenario: docs render + search + resize reflow","description":"Scenario: docs page rendering quality.

Steps:
- Open Docs
- Switch between two docs
- Search for a term
- Simulate resize (send WindowSizeMsg) and verify reflow occurs

Assertions:
- Rendered output changes with width.
- Search navigation finds matches.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in glamour integration and responsive layout.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:39.583157581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:35.534673474Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:34.234331367Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:32.891451084Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-39bb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:35.534642717Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:39.598959258Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mwty","title":"Logs: implement live log viewer (viewport + follow mode)","description":"Implement the Logs page core UI (the thing users will stare at for minutes).

Why:
- Logs are the heartbeat of an ops console.
- This is where styling + performance + interaction quality are most obvious.

Requirements:
- Viewport that can scroll back through a large buffer.
- Follow mode (tail -f) toggle:
  - when enabled, viewport stays pinned to latest entries
  - when user scrolls up, follow pauses automatically
  - a clear UI hint indicates "paused" vs "following"
- Selection (optional but great):
  - highlight current line for copy/export

Performance:
- Must handle thousands of lines without flicker.
- Avoid rebuilding huge strings unnecessarily; prefer rendering only the visible window.

Integration:
- Uses structured log entries + formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`).
- Filtering/search is layered on later but the core viewer should support it cleanly.

Acceptance:
- Smooth scrolling and no flicker.
- Follow mode behavior matches user expectations.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:36.085412578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:29:51.286724849Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:33.789901378Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:36.112421707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-32pp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:36.416170919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
